matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'Duplicate H1 and H2 with near-identical text', 'detail': "An H1 ('Welcome to Vision-Aid') and an H2 ('Welcome to Vision-Aid!') both appear at the top of the page. The H1 should be the sole top-level heading; the H2 duplicate creates ambiguity about the true page title and confuses the document outline.", 'headings_affected': ['Welcome to Vision-Aid (H1)', 'Welcome to Vision-Aid! (H2)']}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - 'Sub-sections present under "Why Customers trust Vision-Aid" section are marked up with incorrect heading mark-up'.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'H1 appears after an H2 in DOM order', 'detail': "The H2 'Welcome to Vision-Aid!' is listed before the H1 'Welcome to Vision-Aid' in the heading sequence. An H1 should open the main content outline; placing it after an H2 breaks the logical reading order for assistive technology users.", 'headings_affected': ['Welcome to Vision-Aid! (H2)', 'Welcome to Vision-Aid (H1)']}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page, 'Primary heading is marked ups with incorrect heading mark-up'.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'Repeated duplicate headings across the page', 'detail': "'Highlights from the last quarter' appears three times as an H2, and several story headings (e.g., 'A Journey of Resilience and Hope: Farhan's Success Story', 'Vision-Aid's collaboration with Christian Medical College (CMC), Vellore') appear twice. Duplicate headings make it impossible for screen reader users navigating by headings to distinguish between sections.", 'headings_affected': ['Highlights from the last quarter (appears 3×)', "A Journey of Resilience and Hope: Farhan's Success Story (appears 2×)", "Vision-Aid's collaboration with Christian Medical College (CMC), Vellore (appears 2×)"]}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - 'Sub-sections present under "Why Customers trust Vision-Aid" section are marked up with incorrect heading mark-up'.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'Sentence-level content used as headings', 'detail': "Several headings contain full promotional sentences or instructional notes rather than concise section labels. These read as body copy or calls-to-action, not as navigational landmarks. Examples: 'Any gift of Smart Assistive Technology will be doubled by a generous matching grant.', 'Please contribute to our Smart Phone and Smart Vision program – every donation will be quadrupled via matching grants from other donors! A signle donation will impact 4 lives!', 'Note:- Email is the preferred and recommended way to contact us.'", 'headings_affected': ['Any gift of Smart Assistive Technology will be doubled by a generous matching grant. (H2)', 'Please contribute to our Smart Phone and Smart Vision program… (H3)', 'Note:- Email is the preferred and recommended way to contact us. (H2)']}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - 'Sub-sections of "A lasting impact through services' are marked up with incorrect heading mark-up semantically.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'Heading used as a link label rather than a section title', 'detail': "'For more details click here' (H2) and 'Sponsors Welcome for the VISION-AID 2023 ANNUAL EVENT . For more details, please click here' (H2) describe an action, not a content section. Screen reader users scanning headings will encounter these with no meaningful context about what content follows.", 'headings_affected': ['For more details click here (H2)', 'Sponsors Welcome for the VISION-AID 2023 ANNUAL EVENT . For more details, please click here (H2)']}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - Multiple links are marked up as heading unnecessarily.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': "Vague heading 'What's New' lacks sufficient context", 'detail': "'What's New' (H3) is too generic to describe its section meaningfully, especially when it appears among many specific story and update headings. Users cannot predict what type of content it introduces.", 'headings_affected': ["What's New (H3)"]}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - 'Sub-sections of "A lasting impact through services' are marked up with incorrect heading mark-up semantically.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'Inconsistent heading levels for similar content types', 'detail': "Some individual stories and updates are marked as H2 while others covering equivalent content (e.g., 'See how the visually impaired benefit from your gift of a Smart Phone', 'Donate A Smartphone To A Blind Student') are marked as H3. This inconsistency implies a false hierarchy where no structural difference exists.", 'headings_affected': ['See how the visually impaired benefit from your gift of a Smart Phone (H3, appears 2×)', 'Donate A Smartphone To A Blind Student And Change Their Life While Doubling Your Impact (H3)']}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page, 'Primary heading is marked ups with incorrect heading mark-up'.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'Duplicate H3 heading', 'detail': "'See how the visually impaired benefit from your gift of a Smart Phone' appears twice as an H3, compounding the duplicate-heading navigation problem noted above.", 'headings_affected': ['See how the visually impaired benefit from your gift of a Smart Phone (H3, appears 2×)']}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page, 'Primary heading is marked ups with incorrect heading mark-up'.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'Typographical error inside a heading', 'detail': "The H3 heading contains the misspelling 'signle' instead of 'single'. Errors inside headings are surfaced directly to screen reader users and reduce trust and clarity.", 'headings_affected': ['Please contribute to our Smart Phone and Smart Vision program – every donation will be quadrupled… A signle donation will impact 4 lives! (H3)']}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - 'Sub-sections present under "Why Customers trust Vision-Aid" section are marked up with incorrect heading mark-up'.

matches.pipeline_issue: Heading Structure: {'issue': 'Flat H2 structure with no sub-sections for a long list of articles', 'detail': "Over 30 consecutive H2 headings represent individual news articles and success stories with no grouping H2 (e.g., 'Success Stories', 'News & Updates') and no H3 sub-headings to create hierarchy. This produces an extremely long, undifferentiated list that is difficult to scan or navigate by topic.", 'headings_affected': ["All article/story H2 headings between 'Highlights from the last quarter' and 'Our Mission'"]}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - 'Sub-sections of "A lasting impact through services' are marked up with incorrect heading mark-up semantically.

matches.pipeline_issue: Landmark issue: {'issue': 'Excessive and duplicate nav landmarks', 'detail': "4 nav landmarks are present. Two share the same aria_label 'Menu' making them indistinguishable to assistive technology users, and two have no aria_label at all, leaving their purpose unclear.", 'severity': 'high'}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - header and footer regions are not marked up semantically.

matches.pipeline_issue: Landmark issue: {'issue': 'Unlabelled nav landmarks', 'detail': '2 of the 4 nav elements have no aria_label or aria_labelledby attribute. When multiple nav landmarks exist, each must have a unique, descriptive label so screen reader users can differentiate them.', 'severity': 'high'}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - header and footer regions are not marked up semantically.

matches.pipeline_issue: Landmark issue: {'issue': 'Duplicate aria_label on nav landmarks', 'detail': "Two separate nav elements both carry aria_label='Menu'. Identical labels on distinct landmarks cause confusion because assistive technologies will announce two landmarks with the same name.", 'severity': 'high'}
matches.baseline_issue: On home page - header and footer regions are not marked up semantically.

matches.pipeline_issue: Landmark issue: {'issue': 'Unlabelled search landmark', 'detail': 'The search landmark has no aria_label. While a single search landmark may be acceptable without a label, adding one improves clarity, especially alongside other unlabelled landmarks.', 'severity': 'low'}
